name: Chaos Theory: Double Pendulum
description:
The double pendulum is a classic example of a complex dynamical system that exhibits **deterministic chaos**. While its behavior is governed by a set of coupled ordinary differential equations, it is highly sensitive to initial conditions ($the$ 'Butterfly Effect').

### The Physics of Chaos
The motion is derived using the Lagrangian mechanics formulation. The state of the system is defined by two angles, $\theta_1$ and $\theta_2$. The Lagrangian $L = T - V$ leads to the following angular accelerations:

$$\alpha_1 = \frac{-g(2m_1+m_2)\sin\theta_1 - m_2g\sin(\theta_1-2\theta_2) - 2\sin(\theta_1-\theta_2)m_2(\dot{\theta}_2^2L_2 + \dot{\theta}_1^2L_1\cos(\theta_1-\theta_2))}{L_1(2m_1+m_2-m_2\cos(2\theta_1-2\theta_2))}$$

$$\alpha_2 = \frac{2\sin(\theta_1-\theta_2)(\dot{\theta}_1^2L_1(m_1+m_2) + g(m_1+m_2)\cos\theta_1 + \dot{\theta}_2^2L_2m_2\cos(\theta_1-\theta_2))}{L_2(2m_1+m_2-m_2\cos(2\theta_1-2\theta_2))}$$

Even with identical starting parameters, a difference of $0.0001$ degrees in the initial angle would lead to a completely different trajectory within seconds. This simulation allows you to explore these parameters and visualize the beautiful, unpredictable path (phase trace) of the lower mass.
